title: Container Threat Models, Users, Capabilities, Seccomp, Rootless, and Daem… · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Capabilities Privileged Devices
summary: A third-party document converter handles hostile files but requests root, privileged mode, every device, and the Docker socket. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker capabilities privileged devices increment in least-privilege container threat model and hardening profile. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Start from dropped capabilities and add only measured needs while treating privileged, devices, host namespaces, and Docker socket access as host authority.
Run as a deliberate non-root UID/GID and reconcile image ownership, mounted files, rootless mappings, and required privileged startup work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Capabilities Privileged Devices · Theory Docker Capabilities Privileged Devices
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory document parser runs privileged as root, mounts daemon authority, writes the image filesystem, has network access, and no resource caps. Remove authority and state the remaining shared-kernel risk.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Capabilities Privileged Devices contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Docker Capabilities Privileged Devices · Theory Docker Capabilities Privileged Devices.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for start from dropped capabilities and add only measured needs while treating privileged, devices, host namespaces, and docker socket access as host authority. For Predict Docker Capabilities Privileged Devices · Theory Docker Capabilities Privileged Devices, keep Docker Capabilities Privileged Devices connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

options.text: Start from dropped capabilities and add only measured needs while treating privileged, devices, host namespaces, and Docker socket access as host authority. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

--privileged is a convenient compatibility switch with only container-local effects. That claim fails the predict docker capabilities privileged devices evidence boundary for Docker Capabilities Privileged Devices.

This does not satisfy Docker Capabilities Privileged Devices for least-privilege container threat model and hardening profile.

Apply that move specifically to Docker Capabilities Privileged Devices while producing least-privilege container threat model and hardening profile.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example document parser runs privileged as root, mounts daemon authority, writes the image filesystem, has network access, and no resource caps. Remove authority and state the remaining shared-kernel risk. Competency probe: Start from dropped capabilities and add only measured needs while treating privileged, devices, host namespaces, and Docker socket access as host authority. A defensible solution applies Docker Capabilities Privileged Devices, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to least-privilege container threat model and hardening profile.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-capabilities-privileged-devices — Start from dropped capabilities and add only measured needs while treating privileged, devices, host namespaces, and Docker socket access as host authority.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-security-least-privilege-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-security-least-privilege-3
    artifact: security-policy.yaml
    decision: worked docker security least privilege 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      user: 10001:10001
      read_only: true
      cap_drop: [ALL]
      security_opt: [no-new-privileges:true]
      tmpfs: [/tmp]
      network_mode: none
    verify: docker inspect document-worker --format json
    failure: Reject privileged mode, daemon-socket mounting, broad devices, root-by-default, or a claim that containers form a hostile-code sandbox.
    repair: Remove daemon authority, choose rootless or user namespaces where applicable, drop capabilities, bound writable paths, and state residual kernel risk.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: --privileged is a convenient compatibility switch with only container-local effects. This fails the Docker Capabilities Privileged Devices contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

# Evidence: docker-capabilities-privileged-devices-4699wl
